title: KitchenCraft Home Made Butter Muslin Cloth for Straining, Cotton, White, 9 cm
features: AN ESSENTIAL FOR HOME BUTTER BAKING: this muslin square is ideal for straining excess buttermilk from your freshly churned butter
FINELY WOVEN: the woven muslin cloth allows buttermilk to drip through, leaving you with rich and creamy homemade butter
VERSATILE: you can also use the muslin cloth for straining fruit and pips from jam, as well as draining whey from yoghurt and cheese
MADE OF 1% COTTON: you don't have to worry about this butter muslin cloth fraying, and it's machine washable for reusability
description: From the manufacturer KitchenCraft Home Made Cotton Muslin Cloth For Straining Butter Useful Information: Measures 90 cm (35½ inches) square Made of 100% cotton Machine washable below 40°C (104°F) There's no need to worry about it fraying, either. It's made of high-quality, lint-free polyester, and you can easily pop it in your washing machine to reuse time and time again. A long-lasting butter-making essential Trying your hand at making your own jam? You won't get very far without a jelly bag for jam, like this one from KitchenCraft's Home Made collection. It makes it easy to strain the unwanted pulp, pips and seeds from your jam. It's the perfect piece of equipment for enthusiasts and beginners. This polyester muslin jam bag is tightly woven to allow those sweet fruit juices to drip on through, while capturing the less desirable bits, so it's ideal for making seedless preserves. Cut to Size Not quite the right size? You can easily cut this muslin jam bag to fit your mixing bowl, saucepan or preserving jar. Not Just For Butter... Make a spice bag This butter muslin strainer can be used to make spice bags, which allow you to add the flavours and aromas of whole herbs and spices to dishes - but without them ending up in the final meal. They also come in handy for mulled wine and cider. Make your own tea bags With a little sewing, you can make your very own reusable tea bags – letting you decide exactly what to put in them and cutting down on waste in turn. Make your own plant-based milk Plant-based milks, like almond, oat and soy, can all easily be made using this muslin cloth. Soak, blend and add flavourings to your chosen base, then use the cloth to strain the milk from the pulp. And much more... It may be designed for butter, but this cotton muslin has loads of other uses. Why not use it to wrap classic sponge puddings before steaming, strain homemade limoncello into bottles for gifting, or remove grains from freshly made kefir? About KitchenCraft Home Made From growing your own, to picking your own, to making your own! Presenting a broad selection of preserving and home cooking items which have stood the test of time, bestowing some of the secret tools many generations could not live without.
